title: velvety sour canadian dark chocolate bake
description: a rich and creamy dessert featuring dark chocolate and a tangy twist, ideal for a post-party hangover or a cozy night in.
instructions: preheat the oven to 400f (200c) and position a rack in the lower third.
roast the hazelnuts in a small pan over medium heat, stirring frequently, until fragrant and lightly browned (about 5 minutes).
let the hazelnuts cool completely, then chop them coarsely and set aside.
in a medium bowl, whisk together the almond milk, espresso powder, and vanilla extract until well combined.
in a separate bowl, melt the dark chocolate chips in the microwave in 30-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
stir the melted chocolate into the milk mixture until well combined.
add the roasted coffee and stir to combine.
pour the batter into a greased and floured 9-inch (23cm) springform pan.
bake for 35-40 minutes or until the edges are set and the center is still slightly jiggly.
remove the pan from the oven and let it cool on a wire rack for 10 minutes.
run a knife around the edges of the pan to release the cake, then transfer it to a serving plate.
